name: Nanshe
description: Nanshe was a Sumerian goddess associated with social justice, prophecy, fertility, and fishing. She was considered the patron deity of Lagash, a prominent city-state in ancient Sumer. As a goddess of social justice, Nanshe was believed to protect orphans, widows, and the oppressed. She judged those who wronged others and ensured fair treatment for the vulnerable. Nanshe was also revered for her wisdom and prophetic abilities; she was said to interpret dreams and offer guidance to those who sought her counsel. Her connection to fertility extended to both agriculture and human reproduction, and she was invoked to ensure bountiful harvests and healthy offspring. As the goddess of fishing, she was believed to control the abundance of fish in the rivers and seas, providing sustenance for her people. Nanshe's temple, located in Lagash, was a center of religious activity and social welfare. Her priests and priestesses played a vital role in administering justice and caring for the needy. Offerings of fish, grain, and other goods were made to Nanshe in exchange for her blessings and protection. She was a compassionate and powerful deity, deeply concerned with the well-being of her followers and dedicated to maintaining order and harmony in the world.
subtitle: Goddess of Justice, Prophecy, and Fishing

keyMyths.title: Nanshe's Judgment
keyMyths.description: Nanshe judged mortals based on their treatment of the vulnerable. She protected those who were wronged and punished those who exploited others.

keyMyths.title: Nanshe and Dreams
keyMyths.description: People sought Nanshe's wisdom to interpret their dreams and receive guidance. She offered prophetic insights and helped them understand the meaning of their visions.
keyMyths.source: Dream interpretation texts from ancient Mesopotamia

keyMyths.title: Nanshe's Bounty
keyMyths.description: Nanshe ensured plentiful harvests and abundant catches of fish. She was invoked to bring fertility to the land and provide sustenance for her people.
keyMyths.source: Agricultural and fishing rituals dedicated to Nanshe

sources.author: Black, J. and Green, A.

sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Mesopotamian deities and their symbolism.
